title: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
gist: Range Rover previewed the GT, its first non-SUV model and the debut vehicle for Jaguar Land Rover's new powertrain-agnostic EMA platform, launching as a full EV positioned against cars like the Lucid Air and Porsche Taycan with a hybrid version to follow.

insights.claim: The Range Rover GT will be the first Jaguar Land Rover vehicle to use the new EMA platform, a flexible, powertrain-agnostic architecture designed similarly in philosophy to the existing MLA platform that underpins current Range Rovers.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Shows JLR building a shared, flexible architecture strategy that can support multiple powertrains across brands rather than designing separate platforms per drivetrain type.

insights.claim: EMA is Jaguar Land Rover's third electrifiable platform alongside MLA (used by Range Rovers) and JEA, the platform Jaguar is developing for its forthcoming Type 01, which is also a four-door grand tourer expected to debut later this year.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Reveals a coordinated platform-sharing strategy across the Range Rover and Jaguar brands, with both brands independently developing grand-tourer models around the same timeframe.

insights.claim: At launch, the Range Rover GT will be a full battery electric vehicle, with the company planning to add a hybrid EV version to the lineup afterward.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Confirms Range Rover is leading its new non-SUV model with a pure-EV launch rather than easing in with a hybrid first.

insights.claim: The Range Rover GT is the brand's first non-SUV model, described by Range Rover managing director Martin Limpert as blending 'effortless EV performance, first-class long-haul refinement and all-terrain capability' in a 'modern grand tourer' format.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Marks a category departure for a brand whose identity has been synonymous with luxury SUVs since 1970, testing whether its brand equity extends to a grand-tourer body style.
insights.quote: We have spent the last few years working obsessively on the fundamentals of the GT formula, reinterpreted in a sophisticated, uniquely Range Rover way... Its blend of effortless EV performance, first-class long-haul refinement and all-terrain capability is pure Range Rover.

insights.claim: The article frames the Range Rover GT as a potential answer to EV grand tourers like the Lucid Air and Porsche Taycan.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Positions the GT as entering an existing competitive segment of premium EV sedans/GTs rather than creating a wholly new category.
insights.quote: Will this be Range Rover’s answer to the Lucid Air and Porsche Taycan?

insights.claim: Camouflaged Range Rover GT prototypes spotted testing at the Nürburgring and the Arctic Circle over the past year were previously misidentified by observers as an all-electric replacement for the Range Rover Velar.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Corrects prior public assumptions about the vehicle's identity, showing Range Rover successfully kept the GT's true nature concealed during extensive testing.

insights.claim: The Range Rover GT's interior deliberately avoids adding a third screen for the front passenger, instead using two OLED screens (main instrument display and infotainment touchscreen) plus a standard head-up display, while still retaining physical controls required by European safety regulators.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Signals a design philosophy that resists the industry trend toward maximal in-cabin screen count, balancing minimalism with regulatory-mandated physical controls.
insights.quote: I was delighted to see that Range Rover’s designers eschewed the idea of a third screen for the front passenger.
